Hsu et al.
"Instructor Strategies for Programming Anxiety"
Programming, Problem Solving, and Self-Awareness: Effects of Explicit Guidance
column4: Introduces a structured approach to teaching programming problem-solving using explicit instruction and six problem-solving stages, along with reflection prompts and context-sensitive help in a coding environment. The study showed improvements in student productivity, independence, self-efficacy, metacognitive awareness, and growth mindset among high school learners.
column5: Relevant to educational technology, programming learning systems, metacognition, and problem-solving skill development. Useful if your research involves learning support systems, intelligent tutoring, or improving programming learning outcomes.

column2: Systematic Review of Machine Learning Techniques to Predict Anxiety and Stress in College Students
column4: Conducted a systematic review of 29 research papers to analyze machine learning techniques used to predict anxiety and stress among university students. Identified commonly used algorithms (SVM, Logistic Regression), important predictive factors (age, gender, financial situation, emotional symptoms), and popular tools (Python).
column5: Highly relevant for research on ML-based mental health prediction, especially for student anxiety/stress detection systems and academic mental health analytics.
